nodes.context: Umbrella term for community-based militia groups in DRC, formed to defend local communities against other armed groups.
Mai-Mai group organized in 2007 by General Yakutumba, active in Kivu provinces, received aid from DRC government.

nodes.standard_name: Mudundu 40/Front de Résistance et de Défense du Kivu
nodes.original_text_name: Mudundu 40/Front de Résistance et de Défense du Kivu (FRDKI)
nodes.context: Smaller Mai-Mai group that cooperated with Rwandan military and RCD-Goma.

nodes.standard_name: Mouvement de Lutte contre l'Agression au Zaïre/Forces Unies de Résistance Nationale contre l'Agression de la République Démocratique du Congo
nodes.original_text_name: Mouvement de Lutte contre l'Agression au Zaïre/Forces Unies de Résistance Nationale contre l'Agression de la Républíque Démocratique du Congo (MLAZ/FURNAC)
nodes.context: Smaller Mai-Mai group that cooperated with Rwandan military and RCD-Goma.

nodes.original_text_name: Nduma Defense of Congo (or Mai-Mai Sheka)
nodes.context: Mai-Mai group formed in 2009 by Ntabo Ntaberi Sheka, active in Walikale Territory, North Kivu.
Mai-Mai group formed by Gédéon Kyungu Mutanga after his escape in 2011, advocating secession of Katanga.
Mai-Mai group commanded by Gédéon Kyungu Mutanga, loosely tied to Mai-Mai Kata Katanga.

nodes.standard_name: Co-ordination for a Referendum on Self-determination for Katanga
aliases: Co-ordination for a Referendum on Self-determination for Katanga
nodes.context: Separatist group composed mainly of former Katanga Tigers, active in Katanga.
Rwanda-allied rebel faction that controlled Kivus during Second Congo War.
Separatist group active in the 1960s in Katanga.

edges.context: Mai-Mai Yakutumba is a specific group under the broader Mai-Mai umbrella.
